clinical_history: The following statements regarding this ECG from an elderly man are true except:
diagnosis: e) The ECG shows a prolonged PR with right bundle branch block (RBBB) and prominent inferior Q waves consistent with myocardial infarction (MI).  The wide notched R waves (RSR' equivalent) in V1-V3 are due to the RBBB.  The rather prominent initial R wave in V2-V3 may be due to posterior MI superimposed on the RBBB.  Echocardiogram confirmed an infero-posterior wall motion abnormality.  RBBB is associated with wide but physiologic splitting of S2.  Paradoxical splitting of S2 (splitting during expiration) is associated with left bundle branch block.
